content: H1 (Comparative Static): ρ increases → equilibrium prices decrease AND share_rate increases. Testable via numerical simulation varying ρ ∈ [0,1] and computing equilibrium outcomes. H2 (Privacy Heterogeneity): High-v users have lower share_rate but suffer greater information leakage; welfare loss is nonmonotonic in aggregate sharing. Testable by stratifying users by v_i and measuring individual outcomes. H3 (Mediated vs. Unmediated): Mediated scheme with de-correlated information yields higher total welfare than unmediated equilibrium. Testable by comparing welfare metrics across two information structures.
